# Implementation Plan: Core Collection Management

**Branch**: `001-collection-management` | **Date**: 2025-11-24 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-collection-management/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/commands/plan.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

This feature provides foundational collection management for a vinyl record tracking application: creating, viewing, editing, and deleting collections. Users can organize their vinyl records into named collections with item type specifications. This is the foundation feature with no dependencies, establishing the core data model and CRUD operations for the application.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: C# 14 with .NET 10 SDK
**Primary Dependencies**: .NET Aspire, Blazor (for web UI), Entity Framework Core (for data access), ASP.NET Core Web API
**Storage**: Azure SQL Database (production), SQL Server LocalDB (development)
**Testing**: TUnit (unit/integration tests), bUnit (Blazor component tests), Playwright (E2E tests), FluentAssertions
**Target Platform**: Azure Container Apps (Aspire-based distributed application), web browsers (Blazor UI)
**Project Type**: Web application (Blazor frontend + ASP.NET Core Web API backend)
**Performance Goals**:
- API response time P95 <200ms for CRUD operations
- Support 1,000 requests/second per service
- Page load time <2 seconds
**Constraints**:
- Maximum 100 collections per user (configurable)
- Memory baseline <512MB per service
- Cold start <3 seconds
**Scale/Scope**:
- Initial target: 1,000 active users
- Average 10-20 collections per user
- 4 CRUD operations + list view

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Test-Driven Development
✅ **PASS** - Will implement TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle; tests written before implementation

### Code Quality & Standards
✅ **PASS** - Using .NET 10, C# 14, following all constitution conventions:
- Blazor for UI (Blazor Server for low-latency)
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- SOLID principles enforced
- Domain-Driven Design: Collection as aggregate root, bounded context for collection management
- Entity Framework Core for data access with repository pattern
- Architecture documentation: Will create C4 Context/Container diagrams, DDD bounded context map

### Comprehensive Testing
✅ **PASS** - Multi-layered testing approach:
- Unit tests (TUnit + FluentAssertions) for domain logic and services
- Integration tests for API endpoints and database operations
- UI component tests (bUnit) for Blazor components
- E2E tests (Playwright) for critical user journeys
- Contract tests for API endpoints
- Target: 80%+ code coverage

### User Experience Consistency
✅ **PASS** - Following UX standards:
- RESTful API design with Problem Details (RFC 7807)
- WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility
- Error messages: Clear, actionable with error codes
- Validation: Immediate feedback, consistent rules
- Response times: <200ms target for interactive operations

### Performance Requirements
✅ **PASS** - Meeting performance targets:
- API P95 <200ms
- 1,000 req/s throughput target
- Memory <512MB baseline
- Will use BenchmarkDotNet for critical path profiling

### Simplicity & Maintainability
✅ **PASS** - Simple, explicit design:
- YAGNI: Implementing only specified requirements
- Minimal abstraction: Repository pattern only for aggregate root (Collection)
- No premature optimization
- Self-documenting code with domain language

### Security, Resilience & Observability
✅ **PASS** - Production-ready practices:
- **Security**: ASP.NET Core Identity for authentication, policy-based authorization, secure secrets management (User Secrets dev, Key Vault prod), input validation
- **Observability**: Serilog structured logging, OpenTelemetry distributed tracing (Aspire integration), Application Insights, health checks
- **Resilience**: Polly retry policies for database operations, circuit breakers for external dependencies
- **Disaster Recovery**: Azure SQL automated backups (PITR 7 days), soft delete for collections (Feature 003), RPO ≤15 min, RTO ≤1 hour

### Cost Optimization
✅ **PASS** - Cost-aware architecture:
- Azure Container Apps with consumption plan (scale-to-zero for dev)
- Azure SQL serverless tier for dev/test, provisioned for production
- Application Insights adaptive sampling (5 items/sec)
- Auto-shutdown for dev environments (6 PM-8 AM weekdays, off weekends)
- Cost allocation tags (Environment, Owner, CostCenter, Project)

**Gate Status: ✅ ALL GATES PASSED - Proceeding to Phase 0 Research**

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-collection-management/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)

    ├── collections-api.yaml   # OpenAPI spec for Collections API
    └── README.md              # Contract documentation
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.AppHost/           # Aspire orchestration
│   ├── Program.cs                            # Service registration
│   └── appsettings.json                      # Aspire configuration
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.ServiceDefaults/   # Aspire service defaults
│   └── Extensions.cs                         # Shared configuration
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Domain/            # Domain layer (DDD)
│   ├── Collections/
│   │   ├── Collection.cs                     # Aggregate root
│   │   ├── CollectionName.cs                 # Value object
│   │   ├── ItemType.cs                       # Value object
│   │   ├── ICollectionRepository.cs          # Repository interface
│   │   └── CollectionDomainEvents.cs         # Domain events
│   └── Common/
│       ├── Entity.cs                         # Base entity
│       ├── ValueObject.cs                    # Base value object
│       └── DomainEvent.cs                    # Base domain event
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Infrastructure/    # Infrastructure layer
│   ├── Data/
│   │   ├── ApplicationDbContext.cs           # EF Core DbContext
│   │   ├── Configurations/
│   │   │   └── CollectionConfiguration.cs    # EF entity configuration
│   │   └── Repositories/
│   │       └── CollectionRepository.cs       # Repository implementation
│   └── Migrations/                           # EF Core migrations
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Api/               # Web API
│   ├── Program.cs                            # API startup
│   ├── Endpoints/
│   │   └── CollectionsEndpoints.cs           # Minimal API endpoints
│   ├── Models/
│   │   ├── CreateCollectionRequest.cs        # DTOs
│   │   ├── UpdateCollectionRequest.cs
│   │   └── CollectionResponse.cs
│   └── appsettings.json
└── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Web/               # Blazor UI
    ├── Program.cs                            # Blazor startup
    ├── Components/
    │   ├── Pages/
    │   │   ├── Collections/
    │   │   │   ├── Index.razor               # List collections
    │   │   │   ├── Create.razor              # Create collection
    │   │   │   ├── Edit.razor                # Edit collection
    │   │   │   └── Delete.razor              # Delete confirmation
    │   │   └── Home.razor
    │   └── Shared/
    │       ├── CollectionCard.razor          # Reusable component
    │       └── CollectionForm.razor          # Form component
    ├── Services/
    │   └── CollectionService.cs              # API client
    └── wwwroot/
        └── app.css                            # Tailwind output

tests/
├── Unit/
│   ├── Domain/
│   │   ├── CollectionTests.cs                # Aggregate tests
│   │   ├── CollectionNameTests.cs            # Value object tests
│   │   └── ItemTypeTests.cs
│   └── Services/
│       └── CollectionServiceTests.cs
├── Integration/
│   ├── Api/
│   │   └── CollectionsEndpointsTests.cs      # API integration tests
│   └── Database/
│       └── CollectionRepositoryTests.cs      # Repository tests
├── UI/
│   ├── Components/
│   │   ├── CollectionCardTests.cs            # bUnit component tests
│   │   └── CollectionFormTests.cs
│   └── Pages/
│       └── CollectionsIndexTests.cs
├── E2E/
│   ├── UserJourneys/
│   │   ├── CreateCollectionE2ETests.cs       # Playwright E2E
│   │   └── ViewCollectionsE2ETests.cs
│   └── PageObjects/
│       └── CollectionsPage.cs                # Page object model
└── Contract/
    └── Endpoints/
        └── CollectionsApiContractTests.cs    # API contract validation
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application architecture selected based on requirements for both UI and API. Using .NET Aspire for orchestration with separate projects for Domain (DDD), Infrastructure (EF Core), API (Minimal APIs), and Web (Blazor Server). This structure follows DDD layered architecture with clear separation of concerns: Domain layer is framework-agnostic, Infrastructure handles persistence, API provides RESTful interface, and Web provides user interface.

## Complexity Tracking

> **Fill ONLY if Constitution Check has violations that must be justified**

No constitution violations detected. All complexity is justified by constitution requirements:
- Repository pattern: Required by DDD for aggregate persistence
- Layered architecture: Required for domain isolation and testability
- Multi-project structure: Standard for Aspire distributed applications

**Status: No unjustified complexity**
